questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student observes two samples under a microscope. Sample A contains cells with a cell wall, chloroplasts, a nucleus, and a large central vacuole. Sample B contains cells with a nucleus, a cell membrane, and mitochondria, but no cell wall or chloroplasts.
Which conclusion is best supported by these observations?

choices.A: Sample A contains animal cells, and Sample B contains plant cells.
choices.B: Sample A contains plant cells, and Sample B contains animal cells.
choices.C: Both samples contain bacteria because both have cell membranes.
choices.D: Both samples contain plant cells because both have nuclei.

questions.correctAnswerText: Sample A contains plant cells, and Sample B contains animal cells.
questions.explanation: Cell walls, chloroplasts, and large central vacuoles are typical plant-cell structures, while animal cells lack cell walls and chloroplasts.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the mass and volume of four objects made of different materials.
Object | Mass (g) | Volume (mL)
A | 24 | 8
B | 30 | 10
C | 45 | 15
D | 50 | 25
Which object has the lowest density?

choices.A: Object A
choices.B: Object B
choices.C: Object C
choices.D: Object D

questions.correctAnswerText: Object D
questions.explanation: Density = mass ÷ volume. Object D has density 50 ÷ 25 = 2 g/mL, which is lower than the 3 g/mL density of the other objects.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A toy car travels 18 meters in 6 seconds at a constant speed.
What is the speed of the toy car in meters per second?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Speed = distance ÷ time = 18 m ÷ 6 s = 3 m/s.

questions.prompt:
A city near the coast often has cooler afternoons than a city farther inland at the same latitude. During the day, land warms more quickly than ocean water. Air above the warmer land rises, and cooler air from over the ocean moves toward the land.
Which process is mainly described in this scenario?

choices.A: A sea breeze caused by uneven heating
choices.B: A landslide caused by gravity
choices.C: A volcanic eruption caused by magma pressure
choices.D: A solar eclipse caused by the Moon blocking sunlight

questions.correctAnswerText: A sea breeze caused by uneven heating
questions.explanation: Land warms faster than ocean water, causing air movement from the cooler ocean toward the warmer land.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A person’s body temperature rises after an infection. The immune system responds by increasing the activity of white blood cells. The body also sweats when the temperature becomes too high.
Which TWO statements describe homeostasis in this scenario?

choices.A: Sweating helps cool the body when temperature increases.
choices.B: White blood cells help the body respond to infection.
choices.C: The body stops using energy during an infection.
choices.D: A fever always means the body has lost all regulation.
choices.E: The immune response prevents all future infections.

questions.correctAnswerText: Sweating helps cool the body when temperature increases. | White blood cells help the body respond to infection.
questions.explanation: Sweating helps regulate temperature, and white blood cells help maintain internal stability by responding to infection.

questions.prompt:
A student wants to test how the temperature of water affects how quickly sugar dissolves. The student uses 200 mL of water, 10 g of sugar, and the same stirring speed in each trial. Only the water temperature changes.
What is the independent variable in this experiment?

choices.A: The amount of sugar
choices.B: The volume of water
choices.C: The temperature of the water
choices.D: The stirring speed

questions.correctAnswerText: The temperature of the water
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor intentionally changed by the student.

questions.prompt: In a simple food chain, grass is eaten by rabbits, and rabbits are eaten by foxes. Grass gets energy from sunlight. In this food chain, grass is the [D1], and foxes are [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=producer; D2=secondary consumers
questions.explanation: Grass makes its own food, so it is a producer. Foxes eat rabbits, so foxes are secondary consumers.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A sealed container holds vinegar and baking soda before they react. The total mass of the container and chemicals is 156 g. After the reaction, bubbles form, but the container stays sealed.
What should the total mass be after the reaction is complete?

choices.A: Less than 156 g because gas has no mass
choices.B: Exactly 156 g because the container is sealed
choices.C: More than 156 g because bubbles add new matter
choices.D: Impossible to predict because all reactions change mass

questions.correctAnswerText: Exactly 156 g because the container is sealed
questions.explanation: In a sealed container, matter cannot escape, so total mass is conserved.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A gardener records the average height of bean plants grown under different amounts of daily light.
Daily light (hours): 2, 4, 6, 8
Average plant height (cm): 7, 13, 19, 24
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: More daily light was associated with taller bean plants.
choices.B: Bean plants grew best with 2 hours of daily light.
choices.C: Daily light had no effect on average plant height.
choices.D: Bean plants stopped growing after 4 hours of light.

questions.correctAnswerText: More daily light was associated with taller bean plants.
questions.explanation: The table shows plant height increasing as daily light increases.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A weather station records rainfall for four months.
Month | Rainfall (cm)
March | 8
April | 12
May | 10
June | 6
What was the average monthly rainfall for these four months?

choices.A: 8 cm
choices.B: 9 cm
choices.C: 10 cm
choices.D: 12 cm

questions.correctAnswerText: 9 cm
questions.explanation: The average rainfall is (8 + 12 + 10 + 6) ÷ 4 = 36 ÷ 4 = 9 cm.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.A: Use the same type of ramp in every trial
tiles.B: Change the height of the ramp
tiles.C: Measure how far the ball rolls after leaving the ramp
tiles.D: Use a different ball material each time

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Change the height of the ramp; T2=Measure how far the ball rolls after leaving the ramp; T3=Use the same type of ramp in every trial
questions.explanation: The independent variable is changed, the dependent variable is measured, and controlled variables are kept the same.

questions.prompt:
In a certain species of flower, purple petals are dominant over white petals. A plant with two dominant alleles is crossed with a plant with two recessive alleles.
What will be the expected petal color of all offspring?

choices.A: Purple, because each offspring receives at least one dominant allele
choices.B: White, because each offspring receives one recessive allele
choices.C: Half purple and half white, because one parent is white
choices.D: A blend of purple and white, because the alleles mix equally

questions.correctAnswerText: Purple, because each offspring receives at least one dominant allele
questions.explanation: A plant with two dominant alleles crossed with a plant with two recessive alleles produces offspring with one dominant allele, so all show the dominant purple trait.

questions.prompt:
A metal spoon feels colder than a wooden spoon at the same room temperature. Metal transfers thermal energy away from a person’s hand faster than wood does.
Which statement best explains why the metal spoon feels colder?

choices.A: The metal spoon has a lower actual temperature than the wooden spoon.
choices.B: The metal spoon absorbs cold from the air.
choices.C: The metal spoon conducts heat away from the hand more quickly.
choices.D: The wooden spoon creates heat when touched.

questions.correctAnswerText: The metal spoon conducts heat away from the hand more quickly.
questions.explanation: Metal feels colder because it transfers thermal energy away from skin faster than wood does.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
In a pond survey, 80 organisms are counted. Of these organisms, 20 are snails.
What percent of the counted organisms are snails?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

At a plate boundary, one oceanic plate moves beneath another plate. This process can produce deep ocean trenches, earthquakes, and volcanic activity.
Which term describes this process?

choices.A: Deposition
choices.B: Subduction
choices.C: Condensation
choices.D: Weathering

questions.correctAnswerText: Subduction
questions.explanation: Subduction occurs when one tectonic plate moves beneath another plate.

questions.prompt:
A student tests whether salt affects the freezing point of water. Four cups each contain 100 mL of water. The student adds different amounts of salt to each cup, places all cups in the same freezer, and records the temperature at which each cup freezes.
Which setup best improves the reliability of the experiment?

choices.A: Use a different freezer for each cup
choices.B: Repeat each salt amount in multiple trials
choices.C: Change both the salt amount and water volume
choices.D: Record only the cup that freezes first

questions.correctAnswerText: Repeat each salt amount in multiple trials
questions.explanation: Repeating trials makes results more reliable by reducing the effect of random error.

questions.prompt: When a flashlight is turned on, chemical energy stored in the batteries is converted mostly into [D1] energy and then into [D2] energy from the bulb.
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=electrical; D2=light
questions.explanation: Batteries provide electrical energy, which the bulb converts mostly into light and some heat.

questions.prompt:
Researchers study a new medicine designed to reduce blood pressure. One group of patients receives the medicine, while a second similar group receives a pill with no active ingredient. Neither group knows which pill they received.
Why is the second group important?

choices.A: It allows researchers to compare results against a control group.
choices.B: It guarantees that the medicine will work for every patient.
choices.C: It removes the need to measure blood pressure.
choices.D: It makes the medicine stronger in the first group.

questions.correctAnswerText: It allows researchers to compare results against a control group.
questions.explanation: A placebo group serves as a control group, helping researchers determine whether the medicine caused the observed effect.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student pulls a cart with different forces and records the cart’s acceleration.
Force (N): 5, 10, 15, 20
Acceleration (m/s²): 1, 2, 3, 4
Based on the table, what happens to acceleration when force doubles from 10 N to 20 N?

choices.A: It decreases by half.
choices.B: It stays the same.
choices.C: It doubles.
choices.D: It increases by 10 times.

questions.correctAnswerText: It doubles.
questions.explanation: Acceleration increases from 2 m/s² to 4 m/s² when force doubles from 10 N to 20 N.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A community tracks changes near a river after trees are removed from a hillside.
Observation | Before tree removal | After tree removal
Average soil loss per storm | Low | High
River water clarity | Clear | Cloudy
Number of small fish observed | 42 | 23
Which TWO conclusions are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Tree removal was associated with increased soil erosion.
choices.B: Cloudier river water may have affected fish habitat.
choices.C: Removing trees caused the river to stop flowing.
choices.D: Fish numbers increased after tree removal.
choices.E: Soil loss stayed the same after tree removal.

questions.correctAnswerText: Tree removal was associated with increased soil erosion. | Cloudier river water may have affected fish habitat.
questions.explanation: Soil loss increased, water became cloudy, and fish numbers decreased after tree removal.

questions.prompt:
A population of insects includes individuals with green, brown, and yellow body colors. Birds more easily see yellow insects on tree bark. After many generations, the population contains fewer yellow insects and more brown insects.
Which explanation best describes this change?

choices.A: Yellow insects changed color because they wanted to survive.
choices.B: Brown insects were more likely to survive and reproduce in that environment.
choices.C: Birds caused every insect to develop the same mutation.
choices.D: The insects stopped passing traits to their offspring.

questions.correctAnswerText: Brown insects were more likely to survive and reproduce in that environment.
questions.explanation: Natural selection favors traits that improve survival and reproduction in a specific environment.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student records how far a sound wave travels in air.
Time (s): 1, 2, 3, 4
Distance (m): 343, 686, 1029, 1372
Which statement best describes the relationship in the data?

choices.A: Distance decreases as time increases.
choices.B: Distance remains constant as time increases.
choices.C: Distance is directly proportional to time.
choices.D: Distance increases randomly with time.

questions.correctAnswerText: Distance is directly proportional to time.
questions.explanation: The distance increases by 343 m for each additional second, showing a constant proportional relationship.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A rain barrel contains 12 liters of water on Monday. By Friday, it contains 18 liters of water.
What is the percent increase in the amount of water?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: The increase is 6 liters. Percent increase = 6 ÷ 12 × 100 = 50%.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A doctor compares resting heart rates for four patients.
Patient | Resting heart rate (beats/min)
A | 62
B | 74
C | 81
D | 69
Which patient has the highest resting heart rate?

choices.A: Patient A
choices.B: Patient B
choices.C: Patient C
choices.D: Patient D

questions.correctAnswerText: Patient C
questions.explanation: Patient C has the highest listed resting heart rate at 81 beats/min.

questions.prompt:
Two students push the same box across a floor. The box moves more easily after wheels are attached to its bottom. The wheels reduce the force that opposes motion between the box and the floor.
Which force is mainly reduced by adding wheels?

choices.A: Gravity
choices.B: Magnetism
choices.C: Friction
choices.D: Buoyancy

questions.correctAnswerText: Friction
questions.explanation: Wheels reduce friction between the box and the floor, making the box easier to move.

questions.prompt:
A scientist studies how fertilizer affects algae growth in pond water. Four tanks receive different fertilizer amounts. All tanks receive the same amount of light, the same starting volume of pond water, and the same starting number of algae cells.
Which factor is being controlled?

choices.A: Amount of fertilizer
choices.B: Amount of light
choices.C: Final number of algae cells
choices.D: Growth rate of algae

questions.correctAnswerText: Amount of light
questions.explanation: A controlled factor is kept the same in all groups. The amount of light is the same for every tank.

questions.prompt: Water evaporates from a lake, forms clouds, and later falls as rain. In this process, evaporation changes liquid water into [D1], and precipitation returns water to Earth as [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=water vapor; D2=rain or snow
questions.explanation: Evaporation changes liquid water into water vapor, and precipitation returns water to Earth as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A circuit contains a battery, wires, and a bulb. A student adds more bulbs in series, one at a time. The brightness of each bulb decreases as more bulbs are added.
Which explanation best fits the observation?

choices.A: Adding bulbs in series increases total resistance in the circuit.
choices.B: Adding bulbs in series removes the battery from the circuit.
choices.C: More bulbs create extra energy in the wires.
choices.D: The bulbs stop needing electric current.

questions.correctAnswerText: Adding bulbs in series increases total resistance in the circuit.
questions.explanation: In a series circuit, adding bulbs increases total resistance, reducing current and bulb brightness.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A class records the number of bacteria colonies growing on plates kept at different temperatures.
Temperature (°C): 5, 20, 35, 50
Colonies after 24 hours: 2, 18, 46, 5
Which temperature produced the greatest bacterial growth?
questions.explanation: The greatest number of colonies, 46, occurred at 35°C.

questions.prompt:
A student claims that increasing the surface area of a solid makes it react faster with an acid. The student compares one large cube of chalk with the same mass of crushed chalk in equal amounts of acid.
Which observation would best support the claim?

choices.A: The crushed chalk reacts completely in less time than the large cube.
choices.B: The large cube and crushed chalk react at exactly the same rate.
choices.C: The large cube produces no bubbles because it contains no matter.
choices.D: The crushed chalk gains mass before the reaction starts.

questions.correctAnswerText: The crushed chalk reacts completely in less time than the large cube.
questions.explanation: Crushed chalk has more surface area exposed to the acid, which should increase the reaction rate.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student investigates whether exercise affects breathing rate. The student measures breathing rate before exercise and immediately after one minute of jumping jacks.
Which TWO changes would make the investigation stronger?

choices.A: Test several students instead of only one student.
choices.B: Use the same exercise time for every trial.
choices.C: Change the counting method for each student.
choices.D: Record only the highest breathing rate.
choices.E: Let each student choose a different exercise.

questions.correctAnswerText: Test several students instead of only one student. | Use the same exercise time for every trial.
questions.explanation: More participants and consistent exercise time make the investigation more reliable and better controlled.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares the average distance of four planets from the Sun.
Planet | Average distance from Sun (million km)
Mercury | 58
Venus | 108
Earth | 150
Mars | 228
Which planet listed is farthest from the Sun?

choices.A: Mercury
choices.B: Venus
choices.C: Earth
choices.D: Mars

questions.correctAnswerText: Mars
questions.explanation: Mars has the greatest listed average distance from the Sun at 228 million km.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.A: Breaks down food and absorbs nutrients
tiles.B: Moves oxygen and nutrients through the blood
tiles.C: Exchanges O₂ and CO₂ with the environment
tiles.D: Sends electrical signals to coordinate responses

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Exchanges O₂ and CO₂ with the environment; T2=Moves oxygen and nutrients through the blood; T3=Breaks down food and absorbs nutrients
questions.explanation: The respiratory system exchanges gases, the circulatory system transports materials in blood, and the digestive system breaks down food and absorbs nutrients.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student heats 100 g of water and records the temperature.
Time heated (min): 0, 2, 4, 6
Temperature (°C): 21, 31, 41, 51
What is the average rate of temperature increase from 0 to 6 minutes?

choices.A: 3°C/min
choices.B: 5°C/min
choices.C: 10°C/min
choices.D: 30°C/min

questions.correctAnswerText: 5°C/min
questions.explanation: Temperature increased from 21°C to 51°C, a 30°C increase over 6 minutes. The average rate is 30 ÷ 6 = 5°C/min.
